title: Packing Codes for Boolean Data
text: Packing Codes for Boolean Data The default packing code for Boolean data is B , but there are a number of other codes available. A packing code might be converted to a more optimum storage format by the DBMS connector interface. For more information, see the relevant connector documentation for your DBMS. Table 1. Boolean Packing Codes Uniface Packing Code Explanation B B is mapped to B2 B1 ASCII Boolean (0|1) B2 ASCII Boolean (F|T) B3 ASCII Boolean (N|Y) B4 Binary Boolean ( 0|1 ) B5 Binary Boolean ( 0|-1 ) How Boolean Values are Interpreted and Stored A field of data type Boolean is interpreted as either TRUE or FALSE in Uniface. The actual value stored in the DBMS depends not only on the packing code of the field, as shown in the table above, but also on the DBMS itself. An empty (NULL) Boolean field will always be interpreted as FALSE, and is displayed by Uniface as 0 , F , or N , depending on the packing code of that field. Boolean data entered in a Uniface form is interpreted as follows: The values 0 , F , f , N , and n are interpreted as FALSE . An empty field is interpreted as FALSE . All other values are interpreted as TRUE . Uniface does not prohibit using Boolean fields as a primary or candidate key. However, for obvious reasons, it does not make sense to do so. A Boolean field can be defined as an index, which is useful in certain circumstances. DBMS Connector Interface In a DBMS that supports the Boolean data type, Uniface Boolean fields will be stored as such. For a DBMS which does not support the Boolean data type, the database connector must remap Boolean packing codes to a suitable alternative, typically a character string of length 1. In this case, the database does not recognize the field as being Boolean. Non-Uniface applications may or may not recognize the field as Boolean, depending on their interpretation of Boolean. When designing the application, you do not need to know of the DBMS-level mapping of a Boolean field, because conversion between database and internal Uniface format is done transparently whenever necessary. Handling Boolean Values in ProcScript The actual value of a Boolean stored in the database varies depending on the packing code used for the Boolean field and on the DBMS. The default packing code for Boolean, B2, corresponds to the interpreted values T and F. However, the B1 packing code corresponds to 1 and 0, and the B3 packing code corresponds to Y and N. To determine whether the field evaluates to TRUE or FALSE, the value must be checked without reference to its actual stored value. Therefore, to ensure that the actual value of the Boolean field does not affect the outcome, use the following code: if (A) Copy Additionally, when doing a ValRep check, the Boolean values must be compared without reference to the actual stored value. Therefore, the values (both Boolean and ValRep value) are normalized to 0 and 1 before the ValRep check is done. Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages. Storage Formats DBMS vendors use the term data type to describe the way data is handled and stored on disk, but this invariably means the way the data is actually stored. In Uniface documentation, the term storage format is therefore used to refer to DBMS data types . A Uniface data type is specified for each field in a modeled entity; a DBMS storage format is internal to the DBMS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
